title: Ensure that the cluster-admin role is only used where  required

input_row.title: Ensure that the cluster-admin role is only used where  required
input_row.description: The RBAC role cluster-admin provides wide-ranging powers over the environment and should be used only where and when needed.
input_row.rationale: Kubernetes provides a set of default roles where RBAC is used. Some of these roles such as cluster-admin provide wide-ranging privileges which should only be applied where absolutely necessary. Roles such as cluster-admin allow super-user access to perform any action on any resource. When used in a ClusterRoleBinding, it gives full control over every resource in the cluster and in all namespaces. When used in a RoleBinding, it gives full control over every resource in the rolebinding's namespace, including the namespace itself.
input_row.audit: Obtain a list of the principals who have access to the cluster-admin role by reviewing the clusterrolebinding output for each role binding that has access to the cluster- admin role. kubectl get clusterrolebindings -o=custom- columns=NAME:.metadata.name,ROLE:.roleRef.name,SUBJECT:.subjects[*].name Review each principal listed and ensure that cluster-admin privilege is required for it.
input_row.remediation: Identify all clusterrolebindings to the cluster-admin role. Check if they are used and if they need this role or if they could use a role with fewer privileges. Where possible, first bind users to a lower-privileged role and then remove the clusterrolebinding to the cluster-admin role : Internal Only - General kubectl delete clusterrolebinding [name]
input_row.impact: Care should be taken before removing any clusterrolebindings from the environment to ensure they were not required for operation of the cluster. Specifically, modifications should not be made to clusterrolebindings with the system: prefix as they are required for the operation of system components.
input_row.default_value: By default a single clusterrolebinding called cluster-admin is provided with the system:masters group as its principal.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_gke_cluster_admin_role_restriction

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
- Use Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) command `gcloud container clusters describe [CLUSTER_NAME] --zone [ZONE]` to identify the GKE cluster and list the associated Kubernetes roles.
- Use the command `kubectl get clusterrolebindings` to enumerate all ClusterRoleBindings in the GKE cluster and identify bindings that provide access to the cluster-admin role.
- Validate the output to check if any principals (users/groups) are bound to the "cluster-admin" role. The command `kubectl get clusterrolebindings -o=custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,ROLE:.roleRef.name,SUBJECT:.subjects[*].name` can be utilized for this.
- Check the list of principals against a predefined set of roles/privileges to ensure that the cluster-admin role is only used where absolutely necessary.
- Clear Pass/Fail criteria:
  - Pass: No unnecessary bindings to the "cluster-admin" ClusterRoleBinding.
  - Fail: One or more unnecessary bindings exist; mitigation actions need to follow the remediation recommendations by reviewing the necessity of each principal.

This approach ensures that the audit of the "cluster-admin" role is carried out efficiently and effectively, using the capabilities of GCP APIs and CLI commands.
